howItWorks.intro: This screen time cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Opportunity Cost = Daily Hours × 365 × Hourly Rate
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much screen time is normal?
faq.answer: US adults average 7+ hours of daily screen time (including work). Non-work screen time averages 3-5 hours. This has increased 50% since 2020.

faq.question: Why does screen time matter?
faq.answer: Excessive screen time is linked to poor sleep, eye strain, neck/back pain, reduced attention span, increased anxiety and depression, and reduced physical activity.

faq.question: What is a healthy amount?
faq.answer: The American Academy of Pediatrics recommends under 2 hours for children. For adults, reducing non-work screen time to 2-3 hours and taking regular breaks is beneficial.

faq.question: How can I reduce screen time?
faq.answer: Use built-in screen time trackers, set app limits, enable grayscale mode, create phone-free zones (bedroom, dinner table), and find offline hobbies to replace scrolling.
